# The $5 Fable — LB Membership Pitch (B126 SCAFFOLD)

**Filed:** B126, 2026-04-25
**Purpose:** Drop-in pitch component shown to a user *immediately after* they experience the Cathedral Effect (cold answer vs. cathedral answer, with their own AI, on their own question). Three registers for three deployment surfaces. Founder-rewrite expected — this is structural scaffolding per [feedback_drafts_as_scaffolding.md](C:/Users/Administrator/.claude/projects/C--Users-Administrator-Documents/memory/feedback_drafts_as_scaffolding.md).

**Canonical ground truth (DO NOT edit without re-checking [project_membership_pricing_identical_for_all.md](C:/Users/Administrator/.claude/projects/C--Users-Administrator-Documents/memory/project_membership_pricing_identical_for_all.md)):**
- $5/yr membership, **identical for every member, forever**
- Lifetime guarantee at signup price
- No tiers, no entry fees, no early-adopter discounts
- 83.3% creator-keeps
- Cooperative-economic structure (Subchapter T, Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge)
- Anchor phrase: *"Same deal for the first member as the five-millionth."*
- Casual register (#42): *"It's your cheese — and we don't move it."*
- Free tier always: Cathedral Effect on public substrate is **free, forever, no membership required**

---

## What the user just saw (the demo)

The user just asked their AI a question two ways:
1. **Cold** — their AI alone
2. **Cathedral** — their AI + LB's substrate

They saw the difference. (R13 empirical: +86.2pp mean lift across cloud vendors. Their specific question may show a smaller or larger lift.)

The fable answers the next question they have: **"OK, this works. What now?"**

---

## Register 1 — In-demo onboarding (post-result screen, ~80 words)

> **Two AIs answered your question. The one that knew the Cathedral got it right.**
>
> That's the architecture. It's free. Always. For everyone.
>
> *(And it's not a cloud-vendor trick — we ran the same architecture on a fully offline Llama 3.1 8B on a consumer desktop, no GPU, no API key, no internet during inference. Cold answer: 0% right. Cathedral answer: 80% right. The substrate does the work, not the vendor.)*
>
> **What $5/yr membership adds:** YOUR stuff in the Cathedral. Your projects, your contracts, your clients, your decisions — all the context you carry around in your head — accessible to whichever AI you use. Permanently. With provenance. Without uploading it to a vendor that may pivot, raise prices, or fold tomorrow.
>
> **You keep what you make.** The $5 you pay today is the $5 your grandkid pays in 2070. Same deal for the first member as the five-millionth. No tiers. No tricks. No catch.
>
> [Become a Member → $5/yr]    [Keep using free →]

[FOUNDER HOOK: this is the moment to tell the story of YOUR cheese — the metaphor in your voice. The bookstore-owner, the moving-the-cheese-out-of-the-fridge, the "you walked in, you walk out with what you brought" framing. Whatever rings truest the morning you write this.]

---

## Register 2 — Op-ed graf (Substack/NYT integration, ~180 words)

The membership exists. It's $5 a year. That number isn't a marketing test or an introductory rate or an "early adopter" wink. It's $5 a year for the first member and it's $5 a year for the five-millionth. There are no tiers. There is no entry fee. The membership you sign up for today is the membership your grandchild signs up for in 2070, at the same price, on the same terms.

The reason it can be $5 is that membership isn't where the value extraction happens — there isn't any value extraction. Members keep 83.3% of what they create on the platform. The cooperative takes the remaining 16.7% as cost-plus-twenty-percent margin to keep the lights on, and the patents you might assume we'd hoard are filed into a Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge that hands them to every nonprofit, cooperative, and academic institution on the planet for free, in perpetuity, forever.

What $5 buys you is your own substrate inside the architecture you just tested. Your decisions. Your projects. Your context — accessible to whichever AI you choose to use, today and twenty years from now, regardless of which vendor wins or pivots or folds.

The Cathedral Effect is free. Membership lets you put your own bricks in.

[FOUNDER HOOK: the "you build the Features — we're building the Board" tagline; the Bridge Builder parable from your father; the "I have learned to wait" register if it fits the piece.]

---

## Register 3 — Substack lede (story-shaped, ~120 words, draws into wider piece)

The demo took thirty seconds. The reader's own AI, the reader's own question, two answers — one cold, one with the Cathedral. The lift is what it is; the empirical mean across cloud vendors is +86 percentage points, but the reader doesn't need the mean. They saw their result.

The next thing they see is the membership pitch, and the membership is $5 a year. Five dollars. Same price for them and the five-millionth member, locked in for life, no tiers, no entry fee, no founder-bird-special with an asterisk attached. The architecture itself is free, always, for anyone — what $5 buys is the user's own substrate inside it. Their decisions. Their projects. Portable to whatever AI is winning twenty years from now.

The *cooperative* version of the AI economy looks like this. It's been here the whole time. Most people just hadn't seen it yet.

[FOUNDER HOOK: the Pine Books / Tiffany Brost anchor if it fits; the "we don't move your cheese" casual landing if you want it warmer; the seven-words / two-words / right-shelf moment as a stitch back to the opening if you used it.]

---

## Constraints (non-negotiable)

1. **Pricing terms identical for every register**: $5/yr, lifetime, identical for first member and five-millionth, no tiers ever
2. **Free tier always exists**: Cathedral Effect on public substrate stays free for everyone, no membership required for the demo or for the architecture itself. Membership = your own substrate inside the architecture
3. **No FOMO, no urgency, no "limited time"**: anti-brand
4. **Recognition ≠ pricing**: if any "Founders List" or "first 10,000" mention appears, it's about *recognition* (named on a page, lapel pin, Pedestal-stake bonus) — never about price
5. **Founder rewrites prose**: scaffold here is structural; expect 60-80% prose rewrite. Anecdote hooks marked `[FOUNDER HOOK: ...]`

---
